properties.remark: MEDIA REPORTING 4 MINUES OF GOLFBALL HAIL AND 3 MINUTES OF PEA SIZE HAIL IN TROUP.
PUBLIC REPORTING HAIL UP TO 2 INCHES IN PORTIONS OF OVERTON.
VIEWER CALLED INTO MEDIA REPORTING THE YARD COVERED IN QUARTER SIZED HAIL

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG FM 330 TOPPLING AND UPROOTING SEVERAL LARGE TREES. ONE HOME HAD MINOR ROOF DAMAGE ALONG WITH MOST OF ITS SHINGLES STRIPPED OFF...WHILE ANOTHER HAD ITS OUTSIDE STORAGE SHED DESTROYED. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE FROM 95 TO 105 MPH...AND THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY TWO MINUTES. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 500 YARDS...AND THE WIDTH WAS 150 YARDS.
TREES DOWN ACROSS FM 330 THREE MILES FROM HIGHWAY 21. MOBILE HOME DESTROYED IN SAME LOCATION.

AN EF3 TORNADO DID EXTENSIVE DAMAGE ALONG ITS PATH INCLUDING DAMAGING 2 BUSINESSES NEAR I-20. SEVERAL HOMES WERE DAMAGED INCLUDING ONE COMPLETELY DESTROYED WITH DEBRIS SCATTERED ALONG I-20. A GROCERY STORE ROOF WAS PEELED BACK AND WALLS FROM A WAREHOUSE WERE BLOWN OUTWARD. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST SOUTHWEST OF BLANCARD LOUISIANA IN CADDO PARISH WHERE IT SNAPPED OR DOWNED NUMEROUS TREES. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE NEAR 150-160 MPH WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 15.6 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 0.75 MILES.
TORNADO REPORTED ON GROUND. TREES DOWN ACROSS INTERSTATE 20.
PEOPLE TRAPPED IN HOMES AND BUSINESSES DESTROYED IN THE VICINITY OF MILE MARKER 628 ON I-20.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG POSEY ROAD...5 MILES WEST OF THE POSEY ROAD EXIT ON INTERSTATE 49...AND THEN TRAVELED NORTHEASTWARD ACROSS INTERSTATE 49. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE TOPPLED OR UPROOTED...ALONG WITH NUMEROUS LARGE LIMBS BROKEN OFF. SEVERAL TREES HAD THEIR TOPS SNAPPED OFF ALONG THE PATH. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE FROM 70 TO 75 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 5 MILES...AND THE WIDTH WAS 150 YARDS WIDE.
PEA, DIME, AND QUARTER SIZED HAIL AT THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 173 ND HWY 1

FM 2493 AND SOUTHERN TRACE CIRCLE...NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED...SHINGLES MISSING FROM MANY ROOFS. ONE HOME HAS ROOF DECKING RIPPED OFF. COUNTY ROAD 1215 AND WALNUT HILL DRIVE...HOMES DAMAGED WITH AWNING THROWN INTO POWER LINES.
ROOF BLOWN OFF OF A BANK AND GROCERY STORE

A WEAK EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN A SUBDIVISION ALONG SOUTHERN TRACE CIRCLE WHERE SHINGLES WERE PEELED OFF SEVERAL HOMES AND A TRAMPOLINE WAS THROWN UP ONTO A HOME. SEVERAL FENCES WERE ALSO BLOWN DOWN. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EAST ONTO WALNUT HILL DRIVE WHERE SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED AS WELL AS MINOR DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES ALONG THE STREET. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE 65-70 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 1.5 MILES WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 75 YARDS.
EF1 TORNADO OCCURRED ALONG CR 2192. A SMALL GARAGE HAD SEVERAL DOORS BLOWN IN AND A SMALL BARN WAS DESTROYED. A LARGE PINE WAS SNAPPED AND OTHER TREES SUSTAINED DAMAGE.
DOWN LINES AND DAMAGE TO GROCERY STORE

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG CENTRAL AVE UPROOTING TREES AND CAUSING MINOR DAMAGE TO AWNINGS ON HOMES ALONG THE STREET. THE TORNADO TRAVELED NORTHEAST AND UPROOTED MORE TREES AND CAUSED MINOR ROOF AND STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO TWO BRICK BUILDINGS AND ONE METAL BUILDING ALONG HWY 154. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST ACROSS HWY 154. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 95 TO 105 MPH...AND THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY TWO MINS. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 0.5 MI...AND THE WIDTH WAS 50 YDS.

AN EF0 TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN WEST OF WEST HEMPSTEAD 40 RD...ALONG A LOGGING ROAD...WHERE IT DOWNED A NUMBER OF TREES AND SEVERAL LARGE BRANCHES. THE TORNADO THEN CONTINUED IN A NORTHEAST DIRECTION WHERE IT CROSSED HIGHWAY 371 KNOCKING DOWN SEVERAL LARGE TREES AND THE POWER LINES ADJACENT TO THE HIGHWAY...BEFORE LIFTING. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 70 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 2.4 MILES WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 50 YARDS.

AN EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN DESTROYING A LAUNDRYMAT AND DAMAGING NUMEROUS HOMES AND OTHER BUSINESSES. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG THE TRACK WHICH STARTED ON THE SOUTHWEST SIDE OF TOWN...WHERE A STORAGE BUILDING WAS DESTROYED. THE TORNADO CROSSED THE TOWN...DOWNING TREES AND POWERLINES. SHINGLES WERE TORN OFF OF NUMEROUS HOMES WITHIN THE TOWN. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST AND RIPPED A 100 FT SECTION OF ROOF OFF OF A CHICKEN HOUSE. MAXIMUM WINDS ARE ESTIMATED AT 105-115 MPH.
POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE. POWER OUT ACROSS THE CITY OF ROSSTEN AND THE SURROUNDING AREA.

AN EF0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN NORTH OF CENTERPOINT ALONG HWY 278. ONE BARN HAD A PORTION OF ITS ROOF REMOVED...AND A SMALL OUTBUILDING SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE. SEVERAL TREES HAD LARGE LIMBS BROKEN OUT...AND A FEW TREES WERE PARTIALLY SNAPPED. MAXIMUM WINDS ARE ESTIMATED AT 60-65 MPH.

*** 1 INJ *** EXCESSIVE HEAVY RAINFALL OPENED UP A SINKHOLE ALONG COUNTY ROAD 1130 THAT WAS NEARLY 20 FOOT DEEP. A VEHICLE WAS SEEN LODGED HALFWAY INSIDE THE SINKHOLE. THERE WAS AN INJURY REPORTED AT THE SCENE.

THE FOLLOWING CITY STREETS WERE CLOSED FROM EXCESSIVE HEAVY RAINFALL. EAST GENTRY PARKWAY AND DUNCAN STREET. BELLWOOD STREET, EAST ERWIN STREET, OLD GLADEWATER, LOOP 323 AND TEXAS COLLEGE ROAD, WEST ERWIN STREET, SHAW AND CHILTON, BONNER STREET UNDERPASS, BROADWAY AND SPRING STREET, DOUGLAS AT FRONT, IDLE AT FLEISHEL, NOBLE, HOUSTON, HURT, WALTON AT REINHEART, BONNER AT LOCUST.
A WOMAN WAS RESCUED FROM FLOODWATERS AFTER HER VEHICLE STALLED ON GLENWOOD ROAD SOUTHEAST OF GILMER. NUMEROUS ROADS REMAINED FLOODED THROUGHOUT THE COUNTY THROUGH THE AFTERNOON.
THERE WERE A TOTAL OF 17 SWIFT WATER RESCUES ACROSS SMITH COUNTY INCLUDING ONE ON HWY. 155 WHERE A MOTORISTS TRIED TO DRIVE ACROSS THE FLOODED HIGHWAY.
SEVERAL ROADS UNDER HIGH WATER IN MOUNT VERNON. OTHER COUNTY ROADS WERE FLOODED AS WELL.

NAPLES POLICE DEPARTMENT IS FLOODED. NUMEROUS ROADS ARE CLOSED IN THE NORTHERN PART OF THE COUNTY. EMERGENCY PERSONNEL ARE PERFORMING SEARCH AND RESCUE AT THIS TIME.

EXCESSIVE HEAVY RAINFALL RESULTED IN HIGH WATER AND A FEW ROAD CLOSURES. THESE ROADS INCLUDED BENTON ROAD AT BECKETT STREET, BENTON AT EAST TEXAS, BENTON AT VIKING DRIVE, HAMILTON ROAD AT EAST TEXAS, HAMILTON ROAD AT BARKSDALE, OLD MINDEN AT PRESTON BLVDL AND SHED ROAD. AN EF0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN SOUTHEAST OF HOPE ARKANSAS ALONG HWY. 278. THE TORNADO WAS ASSOCIATED WITH THE REMNANTS OF TROPICAL STORM HERMINE. A LARGE TREE WAS SNAPPED OFF HALFWAY UP NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF U.S. 278 AND COUNTY ROAD 1060 ALONG WITH SEVERAL LARGE BRANCHES. TORNADO PATH LENGTH WAS 0.25 MILES LONG AND PATH WIDTH WAS 40 YARDS WIDE. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE 60-65 MPH.

AN EF4 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COLLIER AND WATER WELL ROADS. THE TORNADO THEN CROSSED GUM SPRINGS ROAD...WHERE IT COMPLETELY DESTROYED A TWO STORY BRICK HOME. A NEARBY MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED... TWO OTHER HOMES MODERATELY DAMAGED...AND ONE HOME SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE. THE TORNADO SNAPPED NUMEROUS TREES ALONG ITS PATH...AND LIFTED ABOUT 2 MILES NNW OF WINNFIELD. THE MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS WERE 170 MPH...WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 14 MILES...AND A PATH WIDTH OF 400 YARDS.
3 HOMES DESTROYED NEAR 705 GUM SPRINGS ROAD JUST NORTHWEST OF ATLANTA. PRELIMINARY DAMAGE PATH ABOUT 100 YARDS WIDE. NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED OFF ABOUT 15 FEET OFF THE GROUND.

EF1 TORNADO BEGAN IN WOODS WEST OF QUACHITA RIVER AND TRAVELED E INTO FIELD OFF OF HOOTER ROAD WEST. NUMEROUS TREES AND BRANCHES SNAPPED AND TORNADO WEAKENED AS IT MOVED OVER A HOME ALONG HOOTER ROAD EAST. METAL AWNING ON HOME TORN OFF AND DEPOSITED IN FRONT YARD AND OPEN FIELD BETWEEN THE HOME AND LEVEE ALONG OUACHITA RIVER. SHINGLES REMOVED FROM THE HOME. TREE TOPS ALONG LEVEE SNAPPED AS TORNADO TRAVELLED NE AND LIFTED ALONG HIGHWAY 165 IN BOSCOE COMMUNITY. MAXIMUM WINDS 90 MPH WIDTH 100 YDS.
TRUCK STALLED UNDER RAILROAD BRIDGE ON TRENTON ST.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE CONFLUENCE OF THE LITTLE RIVER AND CATAHOULA LAKE IN SOUTHERN LA SALLE PARISH. THE TORNADO REMOVED A FRONT PORCH FROM A TRAILER HOUSE AND DAMAGED THE ROOF OF A BARN ADJACENT TO HWY. 776. THE PATH LENGTH WAS ONE QUARTER OF A MILE WITH A PATH WIDTH OF APPROXIMATELY 50 YARDS.
